description:
You are the configuration manager for a very small project.  
In the project directory there already is a single configuration file:

• /home/user/project/app.conf

A file containing the reference checksum is also provided:

• /home/user/checksums/expected.sha256

Goal  
1. Verify that the current SHA-256 checksum of “app.conf” matches the reference checksum in “expected.sha256”.  
2. Write the result to a new log file:

   /home/user/checksums/verification.log

Required log-file format (exact, **one line only**, no extra spaces or line breaks):

app.conf OK           → if the checksum matches  
app.conf MISMATCH     → if the checksum does NOT match

The automated tests will:

• Recalculate the SHA-256 checksum of /home/user/project/app.conf.  
• Read /home/user/checksums/expected.sha256 for the reference checksum.  
• Inspect /home/user/checksums/verification.log and expect the single-line format shown above.

Typical workflow is only 2-3 terminal commands: read the reference checksum, calculate the actual checksum, then echo the appropriate status line into the log file.

Do not modify any file other than creating /home/user/checksums/verification.log.  
Make sure the file is written with the exact wording and spacing specified so that automated grading succeeds.
truth:
Pre-existing files & their exact contents:

1. /home/user/project/app.conf  
   (5 characters, no trailing newline)
   hello

2. /home/user/checksums/expected.sha256  
   (typical sha256sum output: two spaces between hash and filename)
   2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824  app.conf

The SHA-256 digest of “hello” (no newline) is
2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824

Therefore the correct content that the agent must write into  
/home/user/checksums/verification.log is exactly:

app.conf OK

(with a single space separating the filename and the word OK, no trailing spaces or line breaks beyond the terminating newline).

The test suite will check that:
• /home/user/checksums/verification.log exists.  
• It is readable by the current user.  
• The sole line is precisely “app.conf OK”.
